metadata.title:
WALLACE IN
DANGER Of


BURNING

One Hundred Families Pack
Up Belongings-Appeal


for Help to Spokane.

pages.raw_text:
(By United Press Leased Wire.)


WALLACE, Idaho, Aug. 12-one
hundred families Of Wallace THE
morning are apcking up their be-
longings and will leave for Spokane
and other outside points to escape
the dangers Of tho forest fires
which are raging near, threatening
to wipe out portion Of the city.


With dense smoke enveloping
the city. making 1t impossible to
see a distance Of 200 yards, and
with burning embers falling. car
ried by the wind from the burning
area. Wallace is in immediate dan
ger of destruction.


Everywhere men and women are
running about using every safe
guard to protect their property
from the fire demon. while the city
fire department stands ready to
may be started.


Should the wind become stronger
it is believed that nothing can
save the city. Forest fires raging
in the Placer creek district, five
miles from Wallace, have de-
stroyed property, including COM
pressor buildings, boiler plants,
blacksmith shops and cabins
owned by the Wentworth Clothing
company Of Spokane, the Vienna
International Mining company, the
Arctic Mining company, the Snug
gler Mining company and the Col
den Star company.


On account of the dense smoke
and falling burning embers, 1t is
impossible for the owners of the
properties to get into the camp
and make an estimate of the
amount of damages.


An appeal has been sent to
Spokane for 100 men to aid in
fighting the flames, also the gov
ernment has been asked to send q
squadron of troops from Fort
George Wright. At the present
time there are only so men in the
vicinity of Wallace fighting the
flames.
